hero_subhead: CortexViral's TikTok marketing AI decodes the interest-graph, hooks viewers in the first two seconds, and ships 9:16 video that rides sound trends before they peak. Your brand moves at algorithm speed.

sections.heading: TikTok lives and dies in two seconds
sections.body: TikTok's interest-graph doesn't care about follower count. It samples your video to cold audiences and measures retention at 0.8, 2, and 6 seconds. If viewers swipe before two seconds, distribution dies. CortexViral's Creator team scripts hooks that front-load motion, text overlay, and pattern interrupts. The Intelligence team benchmarks watch-through curves across your niche, then rewrites intros until the algorithm commits traffic. Every concept is stress-tested against the 15-second sweet spot because TikTok rewards completion rate over total view time. Scout agents monitor trending sounds hourly and flag audio with sub-48-hour virality windows. When you plug a product brief into the platform, Cortex generates three hook variants, pairs each with a charting sound, and renders 9:16 video with burned-in captions for sound-off scroll. You ship content the same day a trend breaks, not three days late.
bullets: Hook variants tested at 0.8-second and 2-second retention
Trending audio flagged within 12-hour virality windows
9:16 render with on-screen captions baked in
15-second cuts prioritized for completion-rate boost

sections.heading: Ride duets and audio waves before saturation
sections.body: TikTok distribution amplifies duets, stitches, and native sound adoption. When a song or voiceover enters the exponential climb, you have 24 to 72 hours before the feed saturates. CortexViral's Scout agents scan the Discover tab, creator uploads, and your competitor set every six hours. The moment a sound crosses 10,000 uses with accelerating velocity, Scout flags it and Creator auto-generates three storyboard concepts that pair your offer with that audio. Operator reviews the concepts under your mission parameters, picks the highest-conversion angle, and hands the script to Creator for render. By the time manual teams notice the trend in a weekly report, your video is live and accumulating shares. The platform also tracks stitch and duet opportunities. When a viral video in your category invites response content, Cortex drafts a stitch hook and queues it for your approval or auto-publish if you're running autonomy level three or higher.
bullets: Sound-trend scanner runs every 6 hours across Discover
Auto-storyboard when audio hits 10k uses with rising velocity
Stitch and duet prompts drafted within 4-hour windows
Mission-aligned concept selection before render

sections.heading: Every frame assumes sound-off viewing
sections.body: Sixty-two percent of TikTok sessions start muted or in public spaces. If your message relies on audio alone, you lose two-thirds of cold impressions. CortexViral renders every video with synchronized on-screen captions, keyword overlays, and visual cues that convey the hook before sound kicks in. Creator agents pull transcripts from voiceover scripts, split lines into two-second chunks, and position text in the safe zone above the UI. Intelligence cross-references caption density against watch-through data to find the optimal word count per frame. Operator enforces brand typography and contrast ratios so captions remain readable against motion backgrounds. When you upload raw footage to the Asset Upload Center, Cortex auto-generates captions, applies your brand kit, and suggests three text-animation styles. You approve one and the final 9:16 file exports with captions burned in, ready for Direct Social Publishing. No secondary editing tool required.
bullets: Auto-transcription split into 2-second caption chunks
Safe-zone text positioning above TikTok UI elements
Brand typography and contrast enforcement per upload
Burned-in captions in final 9:16 export

sections.heading: Tags matter less than watch time and shares
sections.body: TikTok's algorithm weights retention and share velocity over hashtag graphs. A video with zero hashtags but 80 percent average watch time will out-distribute a heavily tagged clip at 30 percent retention. CortexViral prioritizes hook strength and completion rate, then appends three to five hashtags that signal category without keyword-stuffing. Intelligence agents analyze which tags correlate with higher share rates in your niche, ignoring vanity tags that drive impressions but not engagement. Scout monitors branded hashtag challenges and flags opportunities when a challenge aligns with your mission. Creator drafts a challenge entry, Operator checks brand safety, and the video publishes with the challenge tag during peak participation hours. The Autonomous Campaign Builder can spin up a seven-day challenge sprint with daily posts, each iterating on the hook that drove the highest completion rate the prior day. Hashtags are appended automatically and updated if the challenge topic shifts mid-week.
bullets: 3–5 relevance-ranked tags per video, zero keyword stuffing
Share-rate correlation analysis per hashtag
Branded challenge entries auto-drafted when aligned
Challenge-sprint campaigns with daily hook iteration

sections.heading: Bulk schedule or auto-publish during FYP peaks
sections.body: TikTok serves the For You Page in regional pulses. Posting at 6 AM, 12 PM, and 7 PM in your audience's timezone increases the chance your video enters the sampling queue when users open the app. CortexViral's Direct Social Publishing module accepts bulk uploads and auto-schedules each clip to the next optimal window based on your historical engagement curve. Intelligence tracks which hours delivered the highest two-second retention over the past 30 days, then weights the queue toward those slots. If you enable autonomy level four, Operator publishes without approval during the top three windows per day. You wake up to posted content and performance dashboards. The system also handles TikTok's three-post daily ceiling. Once you hit three uploads in a rolling 24-hour window, Cortex holds additional videos until the timer resets, preventing platform throttling. Bulk-upload 21 videos on Sunday and the scheduler spreads them across the week at scientifically selected intervals.
bullets: Auto-schedule to top 3 engagement windows per day
30-day retention curve drives time-slot weighting
Autonomy L4 publishes without manual approval
Respects 3-post daily ceiling to avoid throttling

sections.heading: Scout, create, publish, and measure without leaving Cortex
sections.body: Traditional TikTok workflows scatter across trend-monitoring dashboards, video editors, caption tools, scheduling apps, and analytics platforms. CortexViral unifies the entire cycle inside one AI Marketing Operating System. Scout identifies trending sounds and competitor moves. Creator generates scripts, renders 9:16 video, and burns captions. Operator reviews against your mission, then publishes via Direct Social Publishing. Intelligence pulls completion rate, share count, and follower lift into a unified dashboard. You adjust mission parameters like 'prioritize product education over entertainment' or 'test three hooks per sound trend' and all four agent teams recalibrate. The Autonomous Campaign Builder can launch a 14-day TikTok push with daily posts, A/B-tested hooks, and auto-pause rules if cost per follower exceeds your threshold. Every asset lives in the Upload Center with version history. Every campaign traces back to a mission brief. You operate TikTok marketing at L0 for full control or L5 for lights-out publishing.
bullets: Trend scan, script, render, caption, and publish in one system
Mission parameters recalibrate all four agent teams instantly
14-day autonomous campaigns with auto-pause cost guardrails
Unified dashboard: completion rate, shares, follower lift

faq.q: How does TikTok marketing AI identify trending sounds before they saturate?
faq.a: CortexViral's Scout agents scan TikTok's Discover tab, creator uploads, and competitor accounts every six hours. When a sound crosses 10,000 uses with accelerating velocity, Scout flags it and Creator auto-generates three storyboard concepts. You receive the concepts while the trend is still climbing, typically within a 24 to 48-hour window. This lets you publish before the feed saturates and competition spikes. Intelligence tracks how long each sound stays viable, so future campaigns launch even faster.

faq.q: Can CortexViral generate 9:16 TikTok videos with on-screen captions automatically?
faq.a: Yes. Upload raw footage or a product brief to the Asset Upload Center. Creator pulls or generates a voiceover script, splits it into two-second caption chunks, and positions text in the safe zone above TikTok's UI. The system applies your brand typography, checks contrast ratios, and renders a final 9:16 file with captions burned in. You can approve the export or let autonomy level three or higher publish directly. No secondary editing software required.

faq.q: What autonomy levels work best for TikTok posting schedules?
faq.a: Autonomy level two lets you review every video before it queues. Level three auto-schedules approved content to optimal windows based on your 30-day retention curve. Level four publishes without approval during your top three engagement windows per day, respecting TikTok's three-post daily ceiling. Level five runs continuous campaigns, pausing only if cost per follower exceeds your mission threshold. Most brands start at L2, graduate to L3 after two weeks, then enable L4 once they trust the hook quality.

faq.q: How does the platform handle TikTok duets and stitches for viral opportunities?
faq.a: Scout monitors your niche for viral videos that invite response content. When a video crosses your engagement threshold and allows duet or stitch, Scout flags it within four hours. Creator drafts a hook that ties your offer to the original clip, and Operator checks brand safety. If your mission permits reactive content, the stitch or duet queues for approval or auto-publishes under L4 autonomy. You capture trend momentum while the original video is still climbing the For You Page.

faq.q: Does TikTok marketing AI optimize for completion rate or total views?
faq.a: CortexViral prioritizes completion rate because TikTok's algorithm rewards watch-through percentage over raw view count. Intelligence benchmarks your videos against niche averages at the 0.8, 2, and 6-second marks. Creator scripts hooks that front-load motion and pattern interrupts to hold attention past two seconds. The system defaults to 15-second videos because shorter clips achieve higher completion rates. If a concept requires 30 seconds, Creator structures it with a mid-roll hook to sustain retention through the entire runtime.

final_cta_heading: Ship TikTok content that hooks in two seconds and rides sound trends live
final_cta_body: Connect your TikTok account, upload your brand assets, and set your mission. CortexViral scouts trends, renders captions, and publishes to your FYP windows automatically.
